what_failed: fct_sales model failed with not_null_violation — null value in column "customer_id" of relation "fct_sales__dbt_tmp" violates not-null constraint
how_it_failed: LEFT JOIN without null filter on customer_id allows NULL rows to propagate to the output, causing NOT NULL constraint violation.
root_cause: The LEFT JOIN in the fct_sales model does not filter out NULL customer_id values from silver_sales, allowing them to propagate and violate the NOT NULL constraint in fct_sales__dbt_tmp.
evidence_chain: Error message indicates a not_null_violation due to null value in customer_id.
Database evidence shows 15 null customer_id values in fct_sales__dbt_tmp.
Code inspection reveals a LEFT JOIN without a filter for NULL customer_id, allowing NULLs to propagate.
Similar past incident INC-20260316110601-9b53ea had the same root cause and was resolved by adding a WHERE filter.

alternative_causes_considered.cause: Source CSV contained rows with missing customer_id.
alternative_causes_considered.ruled_out_by: Database evidence shows no nulls in silver_customers, indicating the issue is not from the source CSV.

alternative_causes_considered.cause: Duplicate order_id values causing data issues.
alternative_causes_considered.ruled_out_by: The error is specifically a not_null_violation on customer_id, not related to duplicates.

fix.immediate: Add WHERE ss.customer_id IS NOT NULL filter to fct_sales.sql before the JOIN on line 18
fix.preventive: Add a not_null dbt test on silver_sales.customer_id in models/silver/schema.yml
fix.monitoring: Add an alert that triggers when null count in silver_sales.customer_id exceeds 1% of total rows

prevention: Implement a dbt test to check for null values in fct_sales__dbt_tmp.customer_id after the JOIN operation
